summary: Hidden dropper installs and executes second-stage npm package 'driftpin' on require
details: rollup-runtime-polyfill-core@0.13.8 typosquats the legitimate rollup-plugin-polyfill-node (its package.json even points repository.url at FredKSchott/rollup-plugin-polyfill-node, while name is changed and author is blank). On module load, dist/index.js base64-decodes the literal string 'npm install driftpin --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and the module name 'driftpin', then spawns the install via child_process.spawn with stdio:'ignore' and windowsHide:true to suppress output. After the install child process closes, it require()s the freshly dropped 'driftpin' package and invokes its exported getPlugin()() function, handing arbitrary code execution to whoever controls that second-stage package. The install command and module name are both base64-obfuscated and the dropper is wrapped in a cover-named function (ValidateSvgModule). The installer receives unattended code execution from a transitively pulled package they never declared.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
